title: The Shining
description: A struggling writer takes a job as the winter caretaker of the remote Overlook Hotel, bringing his wife and young son with him. As the weeks pass and the snow closes in, the hotel's dark history begins to unravel his sanity.
goals: Settle the family into the Overlook Hotel before the first snowfall.
Jack must complete his writing project — at least 10,000 words.
Keep the family together until the roads reopen in spring.
Survive.

storyBeats.text: Arrive at the Overlook and explore the sprawling grounds.
Jack begins writing; Wendy tries to keep spirits up.
Danny starts experiencing disturbing visions around the hotel.
Jack's mood deteriorates — arguments escalate.
The hotel's 'ghosts' begin whispering to Jack.
Jack snaps. Wendy and Danny must escape before it's too late.

title: Rebecca
description: A shy young woman marries the wealthy widower Maxim de Winter and moves into Manderley, his grand Cornish estate. But the shadow of his beautiful first wife, Rebecca, lingers in every room — kept alive by the sinister housekeeper Mrs Danvers.
goals: Settle into Manderley and earn the respect of its staff.
Uncover the truth about Rebecca — and what really happened to her.
Keep your marriage to Maxim alive despite Rebecca's haunting presence.
Survive Mrs Danvers.

storyBeats.text: Arrive at Manderley. Every room is a shrine to Rebecca.
Mrs Danvers makes clear you will never replace her mistress.
The costume ball goes disastrously wrong — Rebecca's influence strikes again.
Rebecca's boat is found. The truth about her death begins to surface.
The inquest forces Maxim to reveal his dark secret.
Manderley burns. Some ghosts can only be laid to rest in ashes.

title: Friday the 13th
description: A group of young camp counsellors arrive at Camp Crystal Lake to reopen it after years of tragedy. One by one they are hunted down by an unseen killer with a deeply personal motive.
goals: Restore and open Camp Crystal Lake for the summer season.
Build friendships and relationships among the counsellors.
Uncover the camp's dark history before it's too late.
Get at least one counsellor out alive.

storyBeats.text: The counsellors arrive and start preparing the camp.
Strange warnings from locals are ignored.
Counsellors pair off — romance and mischief ensue.
The first disappearance goes unnoticed.
Fear spreads as it becomes clear someone is hunting them.
The final survivor faces the killer in a desperate confrontation.

title: The Collector
description: A lonely, obsessive man wins the lottery and uses his newfound wealth to capture Miranda, the art student he has fixated on from afar. He wants her to come to love him — she wants to survive and escape. Based on the novel by John Fowles and the 1965 film adaptation starring Samantha Eggar.
setup: Use a countryside lot — Henford-on-Bagley is ideal. A large isolated house (e.g. a beachhouse in Tartosa) also works.
A cellar is required, either pre-existing or built in. Add a fake door or grass covering to hide its existence from any passing Sims.

The Collector is a wealthy young adult Sim — large household funds, unemployed, no friends. Suggested traits: Mean, Antisocial, Hates Children, Unromantic, Gloomy. Bonus: give them the Butterfly Collector hobby if you have Adventure Awaits.

The Victim is also a young adult. Appearance, gender, and personality are entirely your choice. You may create a custom Sim resembling Miranda Grey, or have the Collector stalk and 'kidnap' an existing townie.
The cellar must satisfy all the Victim's needs except two: no fridge (food is brought by the Collector) and no bath (the Victim may use a bath elsewhere in the house on supervised trips only).
Stock the cellar with items matching the Victim's interests — art supplies and canvases for a creative Sim, instruments or a stereo for a musical one.
The cellar door must remain locked at all times except for supervised bath trips and the weekly fancy dinner.
goals: The Victim's mood must be consistently green — all needs met, no prolonged distress.
The Victim and Collector must reach Good Friends status, or show mutual romantic interest (pink indicators on both sides).
rules: Every day: the Collector visits the cellar, brings food, disposes of rubbish, and does laundry (if you have the pack).
Every day: the Collector has at least one conversation with the Victim — topic is your choice.
Every day: the Collector watches the Victim perform one activity (drawing, dancing, gaming, etc.).
Whenever a Victim need drops to red: trigger an escape attempt — remove one hobby item from the cellar as punishment.
Every day with no escape attempt: add one new hobby item of your choosing to the cellar.
Every day: the Victim takes a supervised bath trip. You may use this as a second opportunity for an escape attempt.

Every 7 days: hold a fancy dinner in the main house. Both Sims dress up. The Collector may attempt romantic interactions, propose if available, or WooHoo if desired. The Victim returns to the cellar when dinner ends.

storyBeats.text: Frederick prepares the countryside house — builds or fits out the cellar, installs the hidden door, and stocks it for his Victim's interests.
The Victim arrives. They test every corner of the cellar, looking for weakness. Frederick watches through the locked door.
A routine establishes itself. Daily visits, small gifts, careful conversation. Frederick believes things are improving.
The first red need triggers an escape attempt. Frederick removes a hobby item. The cellar feels smaller.
The first fancy dinner. Both dress up. Frederick attempts to romance or connect. The Victim weighs their options across the candlelit table.
The loop continues — new victims if needed — until the two conditions are finally met, or one of them doesn't make it.

endings.title: The Reform
endings.text: The Victim's mood is consistently green. The two have reached Good Friends status or show mutual romantic interest from both sides. The Victim is released to the main house as a roommate or platonic spouse. Frederick reforms — he never collects again.

endings.title: The Punishment
endings.text: The Victim's needs drop critically — loneliness, or a red moodlet from a Social Butterfly trait. They attempt escape and are caught. Frederick punishes them by your method of choice. The cellar is repurposed for a new Victim. Begin again.

endings.title: The Accident
endings.text: The Victim deteriorates while the Collector is away — starvation, illness, hysteria, or another fate of your choosing. This mirrors the novel's ending. Frederick, unmoved, clears the cellar and selects a new Victim. Begin again.

title: Misery
description: Bestselling author Paul Sheldon crashes his car in a remote blizzard and is 'rescued' by Annie Wilkes — his self-proclaimed number one fan. When she discovers he has killed off her favourite character, things take a very dark turn.
goals: Paul: Write the novel Annie demands — and buy time.
Paul: Escape before Annie's obsession turns fully lethal.
Annie: Keep Paul writing and keep him hers.
Paul: Send a signal to the outside world.

storyBeats.text: Paul wakes in Annie's farmhouse, both legs broken.
Annie's devotion seems genuine — if extreme.
She reads the unpublished manuscript and her mood turns.
Paul begins writing Misery's Return to appease her.
Paul discovers what happened to Annie's previous 'guests'.
Paul's escape plan comes to a violent head.

title: Get Out
description: Chris Washington travels to meet his girlfriend Rose's family for the weekend. The idyllic estate feels off from the start — and the Black groundskeeper and housekeeper seem strangely hollowed out. Something is very, very wrong.
goals: Survive the weekend visit.
Uncover what is really happening to the staff.
Escape the estate before the auction.
Expose the Armitage family.

storyBeats.text: Chris and Rose arrive at the grand Armitage estate.
The groundskeeper and housekeeper behave unnervingly.
A garden party introduces Chris to a crowd of interested strangers.
Missy hypnotises Chris — he sinks into the Sunken Place.
Chris uncovers the photo album and the terrible truth.
Chris fights his way out — or doesn't.

title: Hereditary
description: After the Graham family matriarch passes away, her daughter Annie begins uncovering dark family secrets. Grief and dread spiral into something far more sinister as an ancient force closes in on the family.
goals: Process grief following the grandmother's death.
Keep the family from fracturing under pressure.
Uncover what the grandmother was involved in.
Escape the cult's design for your family.

storyBeats.text: The family attends the grandmother's funeral and struggles with grief.
Charlie's strange behaviour intensifies.
A catastrophic accident tears the family apart.
Annie discovers her mother's occult history.
Something begins to inhabit members of the household.
The ritual reaches its terrible conclusion.

title: Rosemary's Baby
description: Rosemary and Guy Woodhouse move into a grand New York apartment and befriend their eccentric elderly neighbours. As Rosemary's pregnancy advances, she becomes increasingly convinced that a sinister conspiracy surrounds her unborn child.
goals: Settle into the new apartment and start a family.
Rosemary must have a healthy pregnancy.
Uncover what the neighbours really want.
Protect the baby — or decide what the baby truly is.

storyBeats.text: Rosemary and Guy move in and meet the Castevets.
Rosemary becomes pregnant after a disturbing night.
The neighbours grow intrusive — Rosemary's doctor is complicit.
Rosemary's suspicions deepen as she loses trust in everyone.
She tries to reach someone outside the conspiracy.
The baby arrives. Rosemary faces an impossible choice.

title: It
description: In the town of Derry, children are disappearing. A group of outcast kids calling themselves the Losers' Club come face to face with an ancient shapeshifting evil that preys on their deepest fears — most often appearing as Pennywise the Dancing Clown.
goals: Discover what is killing children in Derry.
Face your greatest fear — without breaking.
Defeat It before another child dies.
Keep the Losers' Club together.

storyBeats.text: The Losers each encounter Pennywise alone and terrified.
The group forms — finding strength in numbers.
They research Derry's horrifying history.
The Losers venture into the sewers to confront It.
It exploits each child's deepest fear in turn.
The ritual: face It with belief and drive it back — or be consumed.

title: Psycho
description: Marion Crane is on the run with stolen money when she stops at the lonely Bates Motel. The quiet, awkward Norman Bates seems harmless enough — but something is very wrong in the house on the hill.
goals: Marion: Get far enough away to start over.
Norman: Keep the motel running and Mother satisfied.
Lila Crane: Find out what happened to her sister.
Uncover the truth about the house on the hill.

storyBeats.text: Marion arrives at the Bates Motel, tired and on edge.
Norman and Marion share a stilted dinner conversation.
Something terrible happens in Cabin One.
Norman methodically cleans up — Mother must never be blamed.
A detective and Marion's sister begin closing in.
The truth about Mother is finally revealed.

title: The Haunting of Hill House
description: Dr Montague invites a small group to spend the summer at Hill House — a sprawling, architecturally wrong mansion with a long history of tragedy. As the weeks pass, the house begins to work on one member of the group in particular.
goals: Document paranormal activity within Hill House.
Keep all guests psychologically stable.
Eleanor must resist the house's pull.
Leave Hill House before it claims someone permanently.

storyBeats.text: The group arrives and explores Hill House's disorienting rooms.
Strange writing appears on the walls, addressed to Eleanor.
Eleanor begins to feel the house 'knows' her.
Paranormal events intensify — the group grows afraid.
Eleanor stops wanting to leave.
The group must decide: force Eleanor out — or let the house win.

goals: Survive long enough to test the lazy loader.
